title: Evangelion license stickers on merchandise
wikitext: []: Evangelion license stickers on merchandise             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed May 30, 2018 8:12 pm So as far as I can tell there is at least four different license stickers throughout the years of the franchise. There's the NAS (Nihon Ad Systems) stickers from the 90s The overly stylized Gainax logo from the early 00s The more boring Gainax logo which was in use at least until June 2010 And the currently in-use Evangelion Officially Licensed sticker which has been in use since at least 2012 Does anyone know the more exact dates that these switches took place? Most of my Eva merch that's not books is model kits, and there's some looooooooong gaps in model kit releases that make this hard to pinpoint. Another problem with using model kits for this is that the ones regularly in production, like the Bandai ones, can be found with all 4 styles of stickers.

Article: Evangelion license stickers on merchandise
[]: Evangelion license stickers on merchandise             Quote Post by TMBounty_Hunter » Wed May 30, 2018 8:12 pm So as far as I can tell there is at least four different license stickers throughout the years of the franchise. There's the NAS (Nihon Ad Systems) stickers from the 90s The overly stylized Gainax logo from the early 00s The more boring Gainax logo which was in use at least until June 2010 And the currently in-use Evangelion Officially Licensed sticker which has been in use since at least 2012 Does anyone know the more exact dates that these switches took place? Most of my Eva merch that's not books is model kits, and there's some looooooooong gaps in model kit releases that make this hard to pinpoint. Another problem with using model kits for this is that the ones regularly in production, like the Bandai ones, can be found with all 4 styles of stickers.
